div.h2: ET Drain Loop: Correct vs Incorrect Control Flow
div.p: Edge-Triggered mode requires draining the kernel receive buffer to EAGAIN
Root cause of the bug: Kernel receive buffer state after single read:

ul.li: Remaining: 4096 bytes → stranded ✗ In ET mode, the kernel only enqueues an fd in epoll's readiness queue on a state transition (empty → non-empty). If 4096 bytes remain and no new data arrives, no transition occurs → no EPOLLIN. The data waits forever. The client's echo never arrives. The connection is frozen but appears alive. Detectable via: ss -ti showing non-zero rcv_buf.

div.p: Why draining to EAGAIN is mandatory: Kernel receive buffer state across reads:

ul.li: read() call 3: EAGAIN (buffer empty ✓) EAGAIN is not an error — it is the confirmation that you have consumed all available data. Only after EAGAIN can you safely return and wait for the next kernel-triggered EPOLLIN event. The ET contract: one EPOLLIN per transition. Always drain completely.
